items.title: Header interfaces and include guards: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Every function and structure definition should be placed in a header so all source files can share it.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Expose only required declarations and contracts in headers; keep ordinary external definitions and private implementation details in source files.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Expose only required declarations and contracts in headers; keep ordinary external definitions and private implementation details in source files.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.
